items.q: What are Porter's two main generic strategies for competitive advantage?
items.correct: Cost leadership (being the lowest-cost producer) and differentiation (offering unique value customers will pay more for) — Porter argues firms should choose one or the other.
items.misconception: Porter says businesses should pursue both low cost and differentiation at once.
items.random_wrong: Porter's strategies are about marketing channels.
items.why: Porter's central warning is against being 'stuck in the middle'. Students assume more strategies = better; Porter argues choosing one focus is more effective than trying both.

items.q: What four growth strategies does Ansoff's Matrix identify?
items.correct: Market penetration (existing product, existing market), market development (existing product, new market), product development (new product, existing market), diversification (new product, new market).
items.misconception: Ansoff's Matrix is about analysing competitors.
items.random_wrong: Ansoff's Matrix shows market share against profit.
items.why: Students confuse Ansoff (growth strategy) with Porter's Five Forces (competition) or BCG Matrix (portfolio). Ansoff is specifically about the four product/market combinations for growth.

items.correct: Holding minimal stock and receiving inputs only when needed for production — reducing storage costs but increasing vulnerability to supply disruption.
items.misconception: Just-in-time means delivering products to customers exactly on time.
items.random_wrong: Just-in-time means finishing work right before the deadline.
items.why: JIT is about INPUT stock to production, not output delivery to customers. Students misread the term as a delivery promise; it is an internal stock control philosophy.

items.q: Aldi is a textbook example of:
items.why: Correct answer: Cost leadership. “Differentiation” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: Differentiation focus. “Cost focus” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Generic strategy count (with two focus variants)?
items.why: Exact answer: 4. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
